options.description: Utilizing trained senses to read a situation for danger, including finding the nearest exits, evaluating the possibility for an ambush (and if so, from where), and other tactical information.

fields.name: skills.Culture
fields.label: Culture
fields.description: Recalling and understanding information about people and groups, including their history, customs, art styles, cuisines, and other aspects of society. Culture is also learning about languages, including communicating when there is no shared language or quickly picking up enough words to get by.

options.label: Classified Intel
options.description: Representing a focus on military culture, specifically on secret operations, special forces, enemy dossiers, and other need-to-know intel.

options.label: History
options.description: Knowing specific notes or moments in the local history, or the history of a specific culture.

options.label: Linguistics
options.description: Studying languages, allowing characters to learn useful phrases, understand meaning even if there is no shared language, and decipher archaic and dead languages.

options.label: Planetary
options.description: Knowledge of a specific planet or location outside of Earth or Cybertron, as well as its general geography, history, and inhabitants.

options.label: Psychology
options.description: Understanding how the mind works and using that knowledge to predict behavior.

options.label: Specific Culture
options.description: Interest in and study of a particular people.

fields.name: skills.Initiative
fields.label: Initiative
fields.description: Readiness for physical confrontation. Initiative is rolled to mark the beginning of combat and to determine turn order. If there's a question of what happens first when two PCs attempt to act simultaneously, use the Initiative Skill if parties can't come to a mutual decision and a Contested roll isn't appropriate.

fields.name: skills.Science
fields.label: Science
fields.description: Understanding and applying the scientific method, how hypotheses work, and the calculation of the logical path to a definitive answer.

options.label: Medicine
options.description: Knowledge of anatomy, diagnosis, care, and treatment. Cybertronians use Science (Medicine) to save organic lives.

options.label: Specific Subject
options.description: Expertise in an explicit discipline of Science, such as Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Zoology, and more.

fields.name: skills.Survival
fields.label: Survival
fields.description: Getting by in and utilizing wild and dangerous environments. Is that plant poisonous to humans? Does this substance have trace Energon? Did the Decepticons go this way in the forest toward their secret hideout?

options.label: Camping
options.description: Creating a safe, sheltered location outdoors that is protected from the elements and Threats.

options.label: Foraging
options.description: Identifying and scavenging Cybertronian food, Energon, and other supplies from the environment.

options.label: Specific Environment
options.description: Prowess in specific climate and terrain, such as Deserts, Forests, or the Arctic.

options.label: Tracking
options.description: Tracking and following a creature based on signs left behind, from broken twigs to disturbed earth and more.

fields.name: skills.Technology
fields.label: Technology
fields.description: Innovation, engineering, applied mathematics, and the research, experimentation, design, and use of computers and machinery.

options.label: Advanced Technology
options.description: Knowing what to do when faced with unusual and advanced technology, such as high tech equipment found in government science labs, new Cybertronian tech, or even technology from unknown planetary origins.

options.label: Communications
options.description: Experienced with equipment for broadcasting, jamming, unjamming, encryption, and decryption.

options.label: Computers
options.description: Using computer software or hardware for intended purposes or otherwise, including modding, hacking, and more.

options.label: Engineering
options.description: Working out complex physical problems to create, modify, and destroy machines and objects.

options.label: Explosives
options.description: Knowing how to create, arm, and disarm explosive devices.

options.label: Repair
options.description: Seeing to the health and well-being of Cybertronians and the upkeep of vehicles.

options.label: Specific Technology
options.description: Expertise in one particular type of technology, such as artificial intelligence, drones, mechanics, robotics, and more.

fields.name: skills.Animal Handling
fields.label: Animal Handling
fields.description: Caring for, training, and otherwise manipulating animal life. For Cybertronians, Animal Handling applies to all animals, native to any planet, including Cybertronian wildlife.

options.label: Calm
options.description: Make an aggressive animal comfortable in your presence.

options.label: Pet
options.description: Working with an animal companion, although uncommon, to accomplish tasks in concert and communication.

options.label: Training
options.description: Teaching animals behaviors, tricks, or talents through repeated reinforcement. This can be helpful in situations where robotic presence isn't conducive to the mission, but where humans can't be trusted.

fields.name: skills.Deception
fields.label: Deception
fields.description: Lying through direct falsehood, misdirection, body language, and other methods to conceal the truth.

options.label: Bluffing
options.description: Telling outright lies without giving away tells.

options.label: Disguise
options.description: Altering your physical appearance, mannerisms, and voice to pass as someone else.

options.label: Distraction
options.description: Keeping others' attention on you rather than the world around them through misdirection.

fields.name: skills.Performance
fields.label: Performance
fields.description: Any form of artistry, talent, or other creative outlet, like dance, singing, comedy, painting, poetry, cooking, and other artistic pursuits.

options.label: Literary Arts
options.description: Fiction, drama, poetry, and prose.

options.label: Performing Arts
options.description: Dance, music, and acting.

options.label: Visual Arts
options.description: Architecture, ceramics, drawing, mapmaking, painting, photography, and sculpting.

fields.name: skills.Persuasion
fields.label: Persuasion
fields.description: Convincing others to do or see things from your point of view through conversation and body language.

options.label: Diplomacy
options.description: Interacting socially with others in the fashion acceptable for your current situation.

options.label: Embellishments
options.description: The ability to pad the truth with additional information and slight alterations to the facts to make them even more attractive of an option.

options.label: Flattery
options.description: The ability to use sweet words, friendly descriptions, and potentially sycophantic details to make someone consent to your requests.

options.label: Truth
options.description: Using plain and simple facts to make your point of view the obvious choice to follow.

fields.name: skills.Streetwise
fields.label: Streetwise
fields.description: Walking the walk and talking the talk in urban environments, knowing where to go, who to talk to, or more importantly who not to.

options.label: Black Market
options.description: Connecting with and acquiring goods and services outside of traditional means.

options.label: Crime
options.description: Using knowledge and lingo to perceive and identify crime and criminal organizations.

options.label: Gossip
options.description: The ability to seed the street-level populace with a topic and listen for what kind of rumors, lies, and possible clues come back about it over a short amount of time.

options.label: Transportation
options.description: Includes everything from avoiding traffic to the best smuggling routes and even escaping an area or city without being noticed.
